domains.name: Strategic Account Planning
domains.description: Structured planning and orchestration for strategic and key accounts, relationship mapping (org charts, influence and sentiment tracking), white-space mapping (which products bought vs available), joint account plans, mutual close/onboarding plans, QBR automation, and key-account metrics. Distinct from CRM (transactional opportunity record), MA (programmatic engagement), and CPQ (quoting).

domains.name: Accounting Practice Management
domains.description: CPA / tax-preparer practice operating platform: client and engagement management, workflow templates per service line (1040, 1120, 1065, audit, bookkeeping, advisory), recurring-task generation against tax-calendar deadlines, time and billing, engagement-letter and proposal management, secure client portal for document exchange, and capacity-vs-due-date analytics. Buyer is the CPA firm or tax-prep practice, solo to mid-size. IRS Pub 4557 and GLBA regulate the preparer; SOC 2 is commercially expected but voluntary.

domains.name: Agency Management Software
domains.description: Vertical operations platform for advertising, marketing, creative, PR, and media agencies. Combines agency-specific job/traffic management, time tracking with client rate cards, retainer accounting with burn-down, media buying (plans + insertion orders + commission), creative review/proofing, multi-rate billing (cost-plus, retainer, T&M, fixed fee), and per-job profitability. Distinct from PSA (services/consulting firms — no media/retainer/commission semantics), from WORK-MGMT (horizontal, no client-billing or media-buying), and from DAM (asset library only).

domains.name: AIOps
domains.description: Machine-learning-driven event correlation, anomaly detection, and noise reduction for IT operations.

domains.name: Accounts Payable Automation
domains.description: Invoice capture, matching, approval, and payment execution for accounts payable.

domains.name: API Management
domains.description: Full lifecycle API design, gateways, developer portals, security, and monetisation for internal and external APIs.

domains.name: Application Portfolio Management
domains.description: Inventory and rationalisation of the enterprise application landscape, including business capabilities, technology fit, and lifecycle.

modules.name: Portfolio Registry
modules.description: The inventory backbone. Masters enterprise_applications, application_interfaces, technology_platforms and business_capability_maps. The first thing a buyer installs: the structured catalog of what's in the application landscape.

modules.name: Portfolio Rationalization
modules.description: The analyst seat. Masters application_costs, application_value_scores and technology_fit_assessments. Once the registry is populated, this module scores it (TIME), bridges to FINOPS for cost, and drives invest/migrate/eliminate decisions.

domains.name: Application Platform-as-a-Service
domains.description: Code-first managed runtime for deploying web apps, APIs, and background workers. Distinct from LCAP (low-code) and IPAAS (integration).

domains.name: Applicant Tracking and Recruiting
domains.description: Requisition management, candidate sourcing, interview workflows, and offer management. AI-assisted matching and screening overlays are increasingly bundled.

modules.name: Background Checks
modules.description: Pre-employment background-check orchestration with adverse-action workflow. Coordinates external vendor handoffs and gates offer-to-firm conversion on clearance. Requires an external `send_email` tool for FCRA adverse-action notices.

modules.name: Candidate CRM
modules.description: The candidate-relationship backbone of an ATS, masters candidates (including the `prospect` lifecycle state), recruitment sources, agencies, and events. Structurally the same shape as standalone candidate-CRM products. Folds the AI-RECRUIT capability (resume parsing, ML matching, screening assistants) since those tools operate on `candidates` and are tightly bound to candidate workflows.

modules.name: Interviews
modules.description: Interview scheduling, panel coordination, scorecards, and structured assessments. Realizes INTERVIEW-MGMT. Realizes the `interviewing` lifecycle state on `job_applications` (state pruned when this module is not installed).

modules.name: Offers
modules.description: Offer drafting, approval, extension, signature, and acceptance. Realizes OFFER-MGMT. Realizes the `offer_extended` state on `job_applications`. Requires an external `sign_document` tool, drops module Semantius coverage to ~83%.

modules.name: Pre-Employee Record
modules.description: The bridge between offer-accepted and start-date: ATS owns the pre-employee lifecycle stage (paperwork in flight, pre-boarding tasks open, background check pending). Realizes the `hired` state on `job_applications`. Publishes the `pre_employee.activated` event that hands the canonical reconciliation to HCM-mastered `employees`. Formerly NEW-HIRE-HANDOFF, renamed per §7.1 because HCM canonically masters `employees`.

modules.name: Recruitment Pipeline
modules.description: Requisitions → postings → applications with pipeline-stage lifecycle. Realizes REQ-MGMT and CANDIDATE-EXP (application flow slice). Embedded-masters `candidates`, optionally `hcm_positions` and `org_units` for canonical position/org context.

modules.name: Employee Referrals
modules.description: Employee-driven candidate sourcing with referral-bonus tracking (`candidate_referrals`). Embedded-masters `candidates`. Cross-domain handoffs to PAYROLL (bonus payout) and EMP-EXP (engagement signal).

modules.name: Talent Pools
modules.description: Curated candidate groupings for nurture and pipeline-building (`talent_pools`). Embedded-masters `candidates`; deployed alone, materializes a thin candidate shell. Mirrors standalone talent-acquisition CRM products.

modules.name: Hiring Starter
modules.description: Entry-tier deployable for a basic hiring workflow: post jobs, capture applications, run interviews, generate offers. Embeds the canonical masters from the full ATS modules and inherits their lifecycle states. Ships three baseline permissions and one system skill; no workflow gates, no requisition approvals, no background-check orchestration, no pre-employee reconciliation. Upgrades to the full ATS surface without tenant data migration via the embedded-master demotion path.

domains.name: Audit Management
domains.description: Planning, fieldwork, evidence, and issue tracking for internal and external audits.

domains.name: Digital Commerce
domains.description: Storefront, catalog, cart, checkout, and order capture for B2C and B2B digital commerce. Distinct from OMS (post-order distributed fulfillment).

domains.name: Banking Operations
domains.description: Origination, onboarding, servicing, and case-management workflows for retail and commercial banking.

domains.name: Business Continuity Management
domains.description: Business impact analysis, continuity planning, and exercise execution for resilience.

domains.name: Benefits Administration
domains.description: Open enrolment, life-event handling, carrier connectivity, ACA compliance, and benefits decision support.

modules.name: ACA Compliance Reporting
modules.description: 1094-C / 1095-C reporting, affordability tracking, and ACA penalty avoidance. Consumes benefit_enrollments, employees, and legal_entities to produce IRS-bound filings and monthly affordability snapshots.

modules.name: Carrier Connectivity
modules.description: EDI / API connectivity to insurance carriers, 401(k) record-keepers, and equity providers. Masters benefit_carriers and carrier_feeds (834 EDI, custom API, file-drop). Owns the identity-reconciliation problem between per-carrier member IDs and the HRIS employee_id.

modules.name: Enrollment and Life Events
modules.description: The employee-facing workflow surface: elections, dependents, life-event handling, and decision-support tooling. Masters benefit_enrollments, benefit_dependents, life_events; embeds employees for the benefit-eligibility slice (coverage tier, dependent linkage, beneficiary designation).

modules.name: Plan Design and Enrollment Setup
modules.description: The catalog backbone of benefits administration: masters benefit_plans and benefit_open_enrollments, defines deduction codes used by PAYROLL, and scopes plan offerings by legal_entity.

domains.name: Business Intelligence and Analytics
domains.description: Interactive data exploration, dashboards, governed self-service analytics, and embedded analytics. Distinct from observability (OBS) and process mining (PROC-MIN).

domains.name: Business Process Architecture
domains.description: Modeling, governance, and publication of an organization's business processes and capabilities. Includes BPMN process authoring, business capability maps, value stream modeling, reference-framework consumption (APQC PCF, TOGAF, ArchiMate, eTOM, SCOR), process simulation, and stakeholder publication portals. Distinct from Process Mining (PROC-MIN, event-log analytics) and from Application Portfolio Management (APM, application-landscape rationalization), though all three increasingly converge.

domains.name: Computer-Aided Facility Management
domains.description: Light-tier facilities management; mid-market and SMB focus. Desk booking, simple space allocation, maintenance ticketing, visitor management. Typically lighter on utility tracking and lease accounting than IWMS.

domains.name: Cap Table Management and Equity Administration
domains.description: Shareholder ledger, option-pool management, 409A valuations, RSU/option grant workflows, exit waterfall modeling, and ASC 718 stock-based compensation accounting. Primary buyer is the portfolio-company CFO, not the VC firm.

modules.name: Employee Equity Portal
modules.description: Employee-facing portal for grant acceptance, vesting visibility, exercise workflows, and tax-document access. Distinct from board-side grant administration (which lives in CAP-TABLE-GRANTS).

modules.name: Exit Waterfall Modeling
modules.description: Scenario modeling for exit proceeds distribution with multiple preferred-class participation rights, liquidation preferences, and conversion economics.

modules.name: Equity Grants and Pool Management
modules.description: Option pool tracking (size, available headroom), RSU/option grant issuance with board approval, grant acceptance, vesting-schedule administration. Multi-masters equity_grants alongside COMP-MGMT (which holds the compensation-policy slice).

modules.name: Shareholder Ledger
modules.description: Authoritative record of equity holders, security classes (common, preferred, SAFE), and the historical transaction log for the issuing company.

modules.name: Secondary Transactions
modules.description: Tender offers, employee liquidity programs, and shareholder-initiated secondary transfers with board-approval workflow.

modules.name: 409A and ASC 718
modules.description: IRS 409A fair-market-value determination per methodology (income, market, asset, OPM backsolve), and ASC 718 stock-based compensation expense recognition with Black-Scholes or lattice models.

domains.name: Contact Center as a Service
domains.description: Cloud telephony, omnichannel routing, IVR, workforce engagement management, and AI agent assist for contact centres. Distinct from CSM (case-management business app).

domains.name: Customer Data Platform
domains.description: Real-time identity resolution and unified customer profiles across marketing, service, and commerce data. Activates segments to downstream channels.

domains.name: Clinical Device Management
domains.description: Medical-device inventory, preventive maintenance, recalls, and clinical engineering workflows.

domains.name: Contract Lifecycle Management
domains.description: Authoring, negotiation, execution, and post-signature management of contracts.

modules.name: Contract Authoring
modules.description: Template-driven contract drafting with the approved clause library. Masters contract_templates and contract_clauses; produces draft legal_contracts that flow into CLM-NEGOTIATION. Realizes CLM-CONTRACT-AUTHORING and CLM-CLAUSE-LIBRARY-MGMT.

modules.name: Negotiation and Redlining
modules.description: Track-changes negotiation with counterparty exchange, version comparison, and clause-risk detection. Consumes legal_contracts and contract_clauses; gates the in_negotiation -> approved lifecycle transition. Requires sign_document for signature workflow; coverage drops below 100% on the sign_document leg.

modules.name: Obligation Management
modules.description: Register, tracking, and alerting for contract obligations (deliverables, SLA milestones, payment terms). Masters contract_obligations. Publishes obligation.due and obligation.breached events that fan out to OP-RES / GRC.

modules.name: Renewal Management
modules.description: Date-based renewal alerting, auto-renewal-clause detection, and renewal execution. Consumes legal_contracts; publishes renewal.30_day_warning and legal_contract.renewed. Distinct from amendments which run through CLM-REPOSITORY.

modules.name: Contract Repository
modules.description: Executed-contract index. Masters legal_contracts (post-signature) and signature_records. Contributes terms to saas_subscriptions and software_licenses (the buyer-side records that consume contract terms). The system of record for the active, expired, renewed, and terminated lifecycle states.

modules.name: Real Estate Agent (solo / small firm bundle)
modules.description: Solo-agent and small-firm persona bundle. Single-install deployable for individual real estate agents covering lead capture and contact relationships (CRM-LEAD-MGT, CRM-ACCT-MGT), listing creation and MLS syndication, tour scheduling, transaction execution, disclosure handling (RE-BROK-AGENT-OPS), and contract reference (CLM-REPOSITORY). One module to install; larger brokerages deploy the underlying full modules and skip the starter.

domains.name: Configuration Management Database
domains.description: Authoritative record of configuration items and their relationships, underpinning ITSM and ITOM.

modules.name: Baselines and Drift Detection
modules.description: Captures pre-change CI baseline snapshots and continuously compares live state against them, raising drift events for change-management investigation.

modules.name: CMDB Core Repository
modules.description: The authoritative configuration item record, its class definitions, and the relationships between CIs. Houses discovery ingestion, reconciliation, stewardship, and the canonical CI lifecycle.

modules.name: Service Mapping
modules.description: Business-service-to-CI topology projection. Maps applications and business services onto the underlying CI graph so ITSM impact analysis and AIOPS correlation have a runtime-accurate model.

domains.name: Collaboration Governance
domains.description: Inventory, ownership, lifecycle policy, permissions audit, external-sharing controls, sprawl detection, and stale-content remediation across enterprise collaboration suites (document libraries, chat workspaces, file-sync drives, group containers). Buyer is the collaboration-platform administrator (IT operations). Distinct from ECM (the underlying repository market) and from DSPM (data security posture).

domains.name: Compensation Management
domains.description: Compensation planning cycles, pay-equity analysis, bonus and incentive plans, market benchmarking, and equity administration.

modules.name: Benchmarking and Pay Equity
modules.description: Market data ingestion, salary band maintenance, and pay-equity analysis. Masters salary_bands and compensation_benchmarks; surfaces compa-ratio and pay-gap analytics consumed by planning and people analytics.

modules.name: Incentives and Equity Plans
modules.description: Bonus, commission, and long-term equity plan administration. Masters equity_grants and folds INCENTIVE-MGMT + EQUITY-MGMT capabilities (grant lifecycle, vesting, exercise, expense tracking).

modules.name: Compensation Planning Cycles
modules.description: Annual merit, bonus, and equity cycles. Masters merit_cycles, merit_recommendations, and compensation_plans. Drives manager workflows, calibration, approvals, and posting of effective-dated pay changes.

modules.name: Total Rewards Statements
modules.description: Personalized total-rewards statement assembly, delivery, and acknowledgement. Masters compensation_statements; consumes merit_cycle outputs and equity_grant events to compose worker-visible statements.

domains.name: Conversational AI
domains.description: Virtual agents, chatbots, and voice bots for customer and employee self-service.

domains.name: Configure-Price-Quote
domains.description: Product configuration, guided selling, pricing, discount approvals, and quote generation.

modules.name: Approvals and Contract Drafts
modules.description: Discount-approval workflows and contract-draft handoff to CLM. Captures the multi-tier sign-off path that gates high-discount or non-standard quotes from advancing to execution.

modules.name: Product Catalog and Pricing Rules
modules.description: Configurable product definitions, product bundles, and pricing rules. The reference data CPQ quote-building reads from. Includes AI price-optimisation and subscription-pricing variants.

modules.name: Quote Construction and Discounting
modules.description: Sales-quote authoring workflow: quote header, line items, discount application, guided-selling navigation, and quote-document generation. The seller-facing CPQ surface.

modules.name: HVAC Service Management (small-org starter)
modules.description: Small-shop persona bundle for HVAC service businesses (5-30 technicians). Single-install deployable covering customer accounts and contacts (CRM), service dispatch and field execution (FSM-DISPATCH-OPS), installed equipment register and PM cadence (FSM-INSTALLED-BASE), recurring maintenance agreements (FSM-SERVICE-CONTRACTS), quote-to-invoice (CPQ + SUB-MGMT), and parts inventory (HAM). Larger organisations deploy the full modules and skip the starter.

domains.name: Customer Relationship Management
domains.description: Sales force automation, opportunity and pipeline management, account/contact management, and B2B/B2C customer lifecycle. Distinct from CSM (post-sale service) and MA (marketing).

modules.name: Account and Contact Management
modules.description: Masters customer accounts and their associated contacts. The structural backbone of the CRM market: every other CRM module hangs off the account/contact graph. Folds the cross-cutting Customer 360 capability because the unified-customer-view is anchored on the account/contact records this module owns.

modules.name: Sales Activity and Task Management
modules.description: Masters the sales activity log (calls, emails, meetings, demos, tasks) and the touchpoint history on accounts, contacts, leads, and opportunities. Includes the mobile and offline access surface for field sales: the activity-log mobile app is the canonical mobile CRM use case.

modules.name: AI Sales Copilot and Workflow Automation
modules.description: AI-assisted sales workflows: generative copilot, intelligent next-best-action suggestions, automated approval routing, and process automation across CRM. A standalone module because AI copilots and workflow engines are increasingly packaged as a separate paid tier on top of core CRM.

modules.name: Lead Capture and Qualification
modules.description: Captures inbound and prospected leads, scores and routes them, and converts qualified leads to contacts and opportunities. The top-of-funnel module: a CRM deployment without lead management is viable for pure account-management shops, which is why this is a separate module rather than folded into Account Management.

modules.name: Opportunity and Pipeline Management
modules.description: Masters opportunities, the pipeline-stage definitions that opportunities progress through, and the forecasting roll-ups computed from weighted pipeline. The revenue-execution module: where deal management, sales forecasting, and pipeline health analytics live.

modules.name: HVAC Service Management (small-org starter)
modules.description: Small-shop persona bundle for HVAC service businesses (5-30 technicians). Single-install deployable covering customer accounts and contacts (CRM), service dispatch and field execution (FSM-DISPATCH-OPS), installed equipment register and PM cadence (FSM-INSTALLED-BASE), recurring maintenance agreements (FSM-SERVICE-CONTRACTS), quote-to-invoice (CPQ + SUB-MGMT), and parts inventory (HAM). Larger organisations deploy the full modules and skip the starter.

modules.name: Real Estate Agent (solo / small firm bundle)
modules.description: Solo-agent and small-firm persona bundle. Single-install deployable for individual real estate agents covering lead capture and contact relationships (CRM-LEAD-MGT, CRM-ACCT-MGT), listing creation and MLS syndication, tour scheduling, transaction execution, disclosure handling (RE-BROK-AGENT-OPS), and contract reference (CLM-REPOSITORY). One module to install; larger brokerages deploy the underlying full modules and skip the starter.

domains.name: Customer Service Management
domains.description: Case management, omnichannel engagement, and self-service for external customer support.

modules.name: Case Management
modules.description: Customer-facing case capture, triage, routing, resolution, and CSAT close-out. Masters customer_cases and csat_responses. The execution backbone of the CSM category.

modules.name: Entitlements and SLA Management
modules.description: Service entitlements, support tiers, SLA tracking, and contractual support hours. Masters customer_entitlements. Consumes subscription and contract context to bind eligibility and SLA targets.

modules.name: Customer Knowledge Surface
modules.description: Customer-facing knowledge base presentation and authoring. Contributes audience-filtered edits to the cross-cutting knowledge_articles entity mastered by ITSM-KNOWLEDGE; consumes case data so article authoring is informed by support patterns.

domains.name: Dairy Herd Management
domains.description: Herd-level dairy operations: individual-cow lifecycle (calving, lactation cycles, dry-off, culling), parlor management, milk-quality testing, somatic-cell-count tracking, breeding and reproduction records, feed rationing, and FDA Grade A milk regulation compliance. Buyer is the dairy farm operator (large grazing or confined operations) or dairy cooperative member. Workflows are fundamentally different from beef / swine.

domains.name: Digital Asset Management
domains.description: Centralised storage, taxonomy, rights management, and distribution of brand and creative assets (images, video, design files).

domains.name: Data and AI Platform
domains.description: Unified lakehouse-class platform combining storage, pipelines, governance, semantic modeling, analytics, and ML/AI lifecycle under one runtime. Distinct from point-solution data integration (DI), data catalog/governance (DCG), MDM, BI, and data quality (DQ), those domains are features of a Data and AI Platform, not standalone replacements for it.

domains.name: Data Catalog and Governance
domains.description: Metadata management, data discovery, lineage, business glossary, and stewardship workflows for the enterprise data estate.

domains.name: Data Center Infrastructure Management
domains.description: Asset, capacity, power, cooling, and floor-plan management for physical data centres and edge sites.

domains.name: Digital Experience Monitoring
domains.description: Endpoint and synthetic monitoring of the employee or customer digital experience: device health, application responsiveness, and SaaS reachability.

domains.name: Data Integration
domains.description: Bulk and streaming data movement, ELT/ETL, and replication for analytical workloads and data warehouses/lakehouses. Distinct from iPaaS (transactional app integration).

domains.name: Discovery and Service Mapping
domains.description: Automated identification of IT infrastructure and the mapping of components into business services.

domains.name: Data Loss Prevention
domains.description: Detects and blocks unauthorized disclosure of sensitive data (PII, PCI, IP, source code) from leaving the organization perimeter via email, USB, cloud uploads, chat, web, and removable media. Classifies content at rest and in motion; applies policies (block, quarantine, mask, watermark, alert). Complementary to DSPM (which discovers data at rest); DLP enforces at the egress point.

domains.name: Data Quality
domains.description: Profiling, cleansing, standardisation, matching, and ongoing monitoring of data fitness for purpose.

domains.name: Data Security Posture Management
domains.description: Discovers sensitive data across cloud data stores (object storage, data warehouses, managed databases, SaaS apps), maps data lineage and access paths, identifies over-privilege and toxic combinations (PII + over-permissive policy), scores risk posture, prioritizes remediation. Discovers and secures at rest; DLP enforces at egress.

domains.name: Digital Experience Platform
domains.description: Web content management, personalisation, customer portals, and headless content delivery for branded digital experiences.

domains.name: Enterprise Asset Management
domains.description: Maintenance, work order, and lifecycle management for physical plant, equipment, and infrastructure.

domains.name: Enterprise Content Management
domains.description: Document repositories, records management, workflow, and information governance for internal unstructured content. Distinct from DXP (external/customer-facing).

domains.name: Emerging Manager Fund Platform
domains.description: Bundled fund formation, administration, light cap-table, and LP portal as a single architectural product targeting solo GPs and 1-5 partner microfunds. Architecturally bundle-only (components not separately purchasable). Competes against the assembled point-solution stack at order-of-magnitude lower price. Recognized as a distinct market via the 5-criterion bundle-as-market test (see SKILL.md rule #2 amendment 2026-05-23).

modules.name: Lite Cap Table
modules.description: Basic shareholder ledger and grant tracking for the GP entity and portfolio cap-table awareness. Not the full ASC 718 / 409A / exit-waterfall machinery of standalone CAP-TABLE.

modules.name: Fund Formation
modules.description: End-to-end fund-vehicle setup: entity formation, EIN application, banking onboarding, and templated LPA.

modules.name: Lite Fund Operations
modules.description: Simplified LP onboarding, capital calls, and LP portal for single-fund single-class structures. Deliberately less complex than the FUND-ADMIN equivalents; the simplification IS the value engineering.

modules.name: SPV Formation and Administration
modules.description: Single-deal special-purpose-vehicle creation with templated docs and ongoing admin.

domains.name: Employee Experience and Engagement
domains.description: Engagement and pulse surveys, 360 feedback, lifecycle (onboarding, exit) listening, and action planning. Distinct from HRSD case management.

modules.name: Action Planning
modules.description: Survey-result-driven action plans owned by managers or HRBPs. Tracks driver-to-action linkage, completion, and downstream WORK-MGMT task spawn. Closes the listen-act loop for engagement programs.

modules.name: Continuous Listening
modules.description: Engagement, pulse, and lifecycle surveys; question library; response collection; driver scoring. The ongoing voice-of-employee surface for continuous listening programs.

domains.name: Enterprise Performance Management
domains.description: FP&A: budgeting, rolling forecasts, driver-based modelling, scenario planning, workforce planning, and management reporting. Sits above the GL.

domains.name: Core ERP Financial Management
domains.description: General ledger, accounts payable/receivable, fixed assets, period close, multi-entity and multi-currency consolidation, and revenue recognition. The accounting system of record.

domains.name: ESG Management
domains.description: Environmental, social, and governance data collection, disclosure, and reporting.

domains.name: Electronic Signature
domains.description: Legally-binding electronic signature capture, identity verification, and audit trails for digital agreements.

domains.name: Expense Management
domains.description: Receipt capture, expense entry, policy enforcement, corporate card reconciliation, travel-booking integration, and reimbursement.

domains.name: Farmer-Direct Sales Platform
domains.description: Direct-to-consumer (CSA shares, online farm stores, farmers market POS) and farm-to-wholesale (chefs / restaurants / food hubs) commerce platforms for small and mid-scale farms. Provides purpose-built CSA share customization, route optimization for DTC delivery, farmers-market POS reconciliation, and wholesale catalog / order management for the smallholder operator. Captures the small-organic / regenerative / direct-to-consumer segment of the food system.

modules.name: CSA Starter Kit
modules.description: CSA-only subscription management for farms operating a single direct-to-member channel. Embedded shells for memberships, share packs, customers, and pickup locations support membership lifecycle, weekly share fulfillment, pickup-site coordination, and member billing. Upgrade by installing the matching full modules (FDS-CSA-MGMT, FDS-ONLINE-STORE, FDS-HARVEST-PLANNING, FDS-DELIVERY-OPS, FDS-WHOLESALE, FDS-MARKET-POS) as the operation expands into storefront, harvest forecasting, routed delivery, wholesale, or market-day channels.

modules.name: Butcher and Whole-Animal Pre-Sales
modules.description: Cut-sheet templates, whole/half/quarter animal pre-orders, deposit and balance billing, processor coordination.

modules.name: CSA Subscriptions and Member Portal
modules.description: CSA membership lifecycle, weekly share-pack composition, member self-service portal, dietary swaps and pauses, full-season pre-pay and instalment billing.

modules.name: DTC Delivery Routing
modules.description: Route optimisation, driver manifests, ETA notifications, multi-stop sequencing for direct-to-consumer delivery.

modules.name: Harvest Forecasting
modules.description: Yield forecasting per crop and field, projected share availability, member-facing harvest previews.

modules.name: Farmers Market POS
modules.description: Stall-side POS for farmers market days, sale capture across markets, end-of-market reconciliation, multi-market reporting.

modules.name: Online Farm Store and Pickup
modules.description: Public-facing farm e-commerce storefront, on-farm farmstand POS and inventory, pickup-location management, delivery-window catalog.

modules.name: Wholesale and Food Hub Orders
modules.description: Wholesale catalog, B2B order capture (chefs, restaurants, food hubs), multi-farm aggregation, producer payout splits.

domains.name: Cloud Financial Operations
domains.description: Visibility, allocation, optimisation, and accountability for cloud spending across business units.

domains.name: Fleet Maintenance Management
domains.description: Preventive-maintenance scheduling (mileage / engine-hours / time triggers), vehicle work-order management, parts inventory and consumption, mechanic/shop assignment, warranty tracking, and DOT/DVIR inspection workflow. Distinct from EAM (vehicles are mobile assets with telematics triggers; EAM targets fixed plant) and from FSM (FSM dispatches technicians to customers; this dispatches mechanics to internal vehicles).

domains.name: Fleet Management
domains.description: Umbrella for the operational management of vehicle fleets: vehicle inventory and lifecycle, driver assignment, fuel and expense tracking, dispatch, utilization analytics, and regulatory compliance (DOT/IFTA). Cross-industry buyer (logistics, last-mile delivery, field service, public sector, construction, utilities, rentals). Two sub-domains: TELEMATICS (the GPS/ELD/dashcam data layer) and FLEET-MAINT (preventive maintenance, work orders, parts).

domains.name: Farm Management Information System
domains.description: Records, plans, and analyzes farm operations: field mapping, crop planning, planting/application records, harvest tracking, equipment telemetry, yield analytics, work-order assignment, and ag-input inventory. Distinct from generic ERP because operations are weather / soil / season-bound and depend on satellite-imagery, IoT-sensor, and machinery-telemetry feeds (ISOBUS, CAN-bus, John Deere Operations Center API).

domains.name: Food Traceability and Provenance
domains.description: End-to-end traceability of food products through the supply chain: lot / batch genealogy, FSMA-204 critical-tracking-event (CTE) capture and key-data-element (KDE) reporting, supplier-document management, recall execution, and provenance / origin verification. Buyer is the food manufacturer, distributor, or grower-shipper subject to FSMA 204 (Jan 2026 enforcement) and equivalents (EU 1169/2011, Canada SFCR). Regulatory tailwind is the primary demand driver. Differs from generic Supply Chain Visibility by SKU-lot-level granularity and regulator-prescribed event capture.

domains.name: Field Service Management
domains.description: Dispatch, scheduling, mobile work order, and parts management for on-site service technicians.

modules.name: Field Service Dispatch Operations
modules.description: Field-service operational core: work-order creation and lifecycle, technician assignment via dispatch records, field-visit execution, real-time route optimization, and truck-stock parts management. The module the dispatcher and field technicians both live in.

modules.name: Installed Equipment and Preventive Maintenance
modules.description: Asset register of customer-owned equipment the service organization installs and maintains, plus the recurring preventive-maintenance schedules that drive proactive service. Tracks equipment from install through decommission, with PM cadence per unit.

modules.name: Service Contracts and SLAs
modules.description: Recurring service agreements between the service organization and customers, covering specific installed equipment under defined SLA terms. Drives entitlement checks on work orders, renewal workflows, and revenue recognition on the recurring stream.

modules.name: HVAC Service Management (small-org starter)
modules.description: Small-shop persona bundle for HVAC service businesses (5-30 technicians). Single-install deployable covering customer accounts and contacts (CRM), service dispatch and field execution (FSM-DISPATCH-OPS), installed equipment register and PM cadence (FSM-INSTALLED-BASE), recurring maintenance agreements (FSM-SERVICE-CONTRACTS), quote-to-invoice (CPQ + SUB-MGMT), and parts inventory (HAM). Larger organisations deploy the full modules and skip the starter.

domains.name: Food Safety and Quality Management
domains.description: Food-safety management systems: HACCP plan development and audit, hazard analysis, CCP (critical-control-point) monitoring, food-fraud / VACCP / TACCP analysis, GFSI scheme certification audits (SQF, BRC-GS, FSSC 22000, IFS), allergen control, sanitation verification, environmental monitoring program (EMP), and recall management. Buyer is the food manufacturer subject to FSMA Preventive Controls Rule and (for export) GFSI-equivalent certification. Heavy overlap with Food Traceability on recall execution and supplier-document management; FSQM is the broader safety-system-of-record while Traceability is the lot-genealogy / CTE-capture specialist.

domains.name: Private Capital Fund Administration
domains.description: Fund accounting, capital calls, distribution waterfalls, LP onboarding (KYC/AML), partner capital account (PCAP) statements, and LP-facing reporting for private capital funds (VC, PE, private credit, real estate).

modules.name: Capital Calls
modules.description: Capital-call notice generation, LP payment tracking, and call schedule management across funds. Requires approval workflow (GP sign-off, sometimes IC).

modules.name: Distributions and Waterfalls
modules.description: Distribution notice generation with European/American/hybrid waterfall calculations, catchup, clawback, and hurdle-rate handling.

modules.name: Fund Ledger
modules.description: Fund accounting: GP books for the fund vehicle, multi-currency support, GAAP/IFRS partnership accounting, and audit-support packaging. Masters funds, fund ledger entries, and close periods.

modules.name: LP Commitments
modules.description: LP onboarding (KYC/AML + subscription document collection), PCAP (partner capital account) statement generation, and LP-facing portal for statement and document access.

domains.name: Governance, Risk and Compliance
domains.description: Integrated risk management spanning policy, controls, risk assessment, and compliance evidence.

domains.name: Go-to-Market Planning
domains.description: Cross-functional planning of how to enter and grow markets, ICP and segmentation, territory carving, account scoring, channel mix, sales capacity modeling, and launch orchestration. Sits across Sales Ops, Marketing Ops, and Customer Success Ops. Distinct from SALES-PERF (which executes the territory/quota design once decided), EPM (finance-led), and SWP (workforce-led).

domains.name: Hardware Asset Management
domains.description: Inventory, lifecycle, and disposal management of physical IT hardware assets.

modules.name: Hardware Asset Registry
modules.description: Authoritative register of hardware assets and their reference models. Tracks discovery feeds, lifecycle states, financial attributes (purchase, depreciation), and ownership.

modules.name: Warranty, Disposal, and Spare Parts
modules.description: Warranty registrations, end-of-life disposal records, and spare-parts inventory at warehouses and on technician trucks. Sits adjacent to the asset registry; feeds field-service operations.

modules.name: HVAC Service Management (small-org starter)
modules.description: Small-shop persona bundle for HVAC service businesses (5-30 technicians). Single-install deployable covering customer accounts and contacts (CRM), service dispatch and field execution (FSM-DISPATCH-OPS), installed equipment register and PM cadence (FSM-INSTALLED-BASE), recurring maintenance agreements (FSM-SERVICE-CONTRACTS), quote-to-invoice (CPQ + SUB-MGMT), and parts inventory (HAM). Larger organisations deploy the full modules and skip the starter.

domains.name: Healthcare Patient Operations
domains.description: Patient scheduling, care coordination, and clinical workflow management for providers.

domains.name: Human Capital Management
domains.description: Core HR system of record covering worker data, organisation and position management, self-service, and lifecycle workflows. The HRIS / system-of-record layer of the HR stack.

modules.name: Core Worker Record
modules.description: Core worker record: identity, employment lifecycle, contracts, and effective-dated employment events. The system-of-record layer for every employee.

modules.name: Employee Lifecycle Workflows
modules.description: Employee and manager self-service portal coordinating cross-domain lifecycle: onboarding intake from ATS, lifecycle change requests, leave/absence visibility, and offboarding handoffs to ITSM/IGA/PAYROLL. Workflow surface on top of HCM-CORE-WORKER and HCM-ORG-POSITIONS, no masters of its own.

modules.name: Organisation and Position Management
modules.description: Organisation design: org units, positions, and job profiles. Effective-dated org structure with position approval workflow, job-profile authority, and the hierarchical backbone every downstream HR system reads. Foundation for workforce planning and span-of-control analytics.

domains.name: Headless Content Management
domains.description: API-first content platforms and headless CMS for omnichannel content delivery, decoupled from presentation.

domains.name: HR Service Delivery
domains.description: Employee case management, knowledge, journeys, and document management for the HR function.

modules.name: HR Case Management
modules.description: Core HR case management surface: HR cases, case taxonomy, triage, approval routing, and AI-assisted classification. Masters hr_cases and case_categories. The execution backbone of any HRSD deployment.

modules.name: Employee Self-Service Portal
modules.description: Employee self-service intake surface: catalog browse, request submission, knowledge search, and case status. Workflow layer on top of HRSD-CASE-MGMT, no masters of its own. Hands intake off to case-mgmt and can route IT-flavored requests through to ITSM.

modules.name: HR Knowledge
modules.description: HR knowledge surface: HR-audience articles, FAQs, and policy explainers. Consumes the cross-cutting knowledge_articles entity (mastered by ITSM/KMS) and feeds back authoring suggestions when cases are resolved.

domains.name: Intelligent Document Processing
domains.description: Capture, classification, extraction, and validation of structured and unstructured documents.

domains.name: Identity Governance and Administration
domains.description: Joiner-mover-leaver workflows, access reviews, segregation-of-duties, and entitlement governance.

modules.name: IGA Access Certification
modules.description: Scheduled and ad-hoc access recertification campaigns. Reviewers attest or revoke; revoke decisions drive provisioning; campaign closure produces GRC evidence.

modules.name: IGA Access Request
modules.description: Joiner/mover/ad-hoc access request workflow: request submission, SoD pre-check, approval routing, provisioning handoff.

modules.name: IGA Automated Provisioning
modules.description: Connector framework + provisioning event log. Executes create/modify/suspend/delete actions in downstream systems on receipt of approved requests or JML events.

modules.name: IGA Entitlement Catalog
modules.description: Catalog of access primitives (roles, groups, permission sets, birthright bundles). Lightweight lifecycle: draft -> published -> deprecated -> retired.

modules.name: IGA Segregation of Duties Management
modules.description: Toxic-combination ruleset, detection engine, triage workflow, mitigation tracking, accepted-risk register.

domains.name: Insurance Claims Management
domains.description: First notice of loss, adjudication, settlement, and recovery workflows for P&C and life insurance.

domains.name: Employee Communications & Intranet
domains.description: Modern intranet platforms, internal news and announcement broadcasting, targeted employee communications, frontline / deskless worker engagement, federated employee search, and onboarding / journey orchestration. Buyer is Internal Comms or HR. Distinct from Knowledge Management (knowledge artifacts), Workstream Collaboration (chat), HR Service Delivery (case management), and Employee Experience (engagement listening / surveys).

domains.name: Investor Relationship and Deal Flow Management
domains.description: Relationship-intelligence CRM and deal-pipeline software for investors (VC, PE, family offices). Tracks founders, executives, deals, portfolio companies, and LP relationships with auto-network inference from email and calendar metadata.

modules.name: Deal Pipeline
modules.description: The investment-opportunity pipeline backbone: deal sourcing through closure, deal-team collaboration, and IC investment memos. Structurally similar to standalone deal-flow products. Masters deals and investment memos.

modules.name: LP CRM
modules.description: Fundraising-side CRM for LP outreach, meetings, and capital-commitment pipeline. Also lightweight portfolio-company awareness for deal-team consumption (full portfolio monitoring lives in PORT-MONIT).

modules.name: Relationship Graph
modules.description: Auto-inferred relationship graph from email/calendar metadata with warm-introduction path computation across the firm's network. Identity reconciliation of founders, executives, and co-investors across deal stages.

domains.name: Inventory Management
domains.description: SMB and mid-market multi-location stock control, lot and serial tracking, reorder rules, ABC classification, and stock-movement reconciliation. Distinct from WMS (warehouse execution: picking, packing, wave planning) and from SCP (demand forecasting, MRP).

modules.name: Core Stock Tracking
modules.description: Items, balances, lot and serial tracking, stock movements, transfers, and cycle counts. The deployable base module of INV-MGMT.

modules.name: Kitting and Light Assembly
modules.description: Kit definitions, kit explosion at order or production time, and finished-kit on-hand maintenance. Add-on for sellers with bundled-SKU or light-manufacturing flows.

modules.name: Replenishment and Reorder Automation
modules.description: Reorder rules, ABC-XYZ classification, and available-to-promise calculation. Layered on top of Core Stock to drive automated replenishment.

domains.name: Integration Platform as a Service
domains.description: Cloud-native iPaaS for integrating SaaS and on-prem applications via prebuilt connectors and flows.

domains.name: IT Asset Management
domains.description: Umbrella domain covering hardware, software, cloud-cost, and enterprise-asset management.

modules.name: Cross-Asset Contract Management
modules.description: Unified contract management for agreements spanning multiple asset types. Masters asset_contracts at the umbrella layer because a single contract often covers hardware leases plus software licenses plus SaaS bundles plus cloud commitments.

modules.name: Unified Asset Lifecycle Log
modules.description: Cross-asset event log capturing acquisition, assignment, transfer, and retirement across all asset types. Masters asset_lifecycle_events as the single source of truth for the asset audit trail.

modules.name: Asset Normalization and Catalog
modules.description: Cross-asset reference-data layer: model recognition, software-title normalization, SaaS app catalog, and identity reconciliation across HAM, SAM, SMP, and FINOPS. The substrate that turns disparate asset feeds into a unified portfolio. No own masters in v1; reference data lives in third-party taxonomies. Computed/reconciliation layer over HAM/SAM/SMP/FINOPS/EAM masters; intentionally has no own data_objects.

modules.name: Portfolio TCO Reporting
modules.description: Total cost of ownership and portfolio-level reporting rolling up HAM, SAM, SMP, FINOPS, and EAM. Derived module: no own masters, consumes from every sub-domain and the cross-cutting umbrella modules.

domains.name: IT Operations Management
domains.description: Monitoring, event management, and operational control of IT infrastructure and services.

domains.name: IT Service Management
domains.description: Workflows for managing IT requests, incidents, problems, changes, and knowledge in support of internal business users.

modules.name: Agent Workspace and Reporting
modules.description: Unified agent experience: workspace, walk-up support, virtual agent / conversational ITSM, and continuous-improvement reporting.

modules.name: Change and Release Management
modules.description: Change request capture, CAB workflow, approval routing, release planning, and standard-change catalog.

modules.name: Event and Alert Handling
modules.description: Reactive event filtering, deduplication, and routing into the incident workflow. Bridge between observability platforms and ITSM.

modules.name: Incident Management
modules.description: Capture, triage, route, and resolve service-disrupting incidents. Includes major-incident command, AI-assisted classification, and skill-based assignment.

modules.name: Knowledge Management
modules.description: Authoring, review, publication, and consumption of knowledge articles supporting ITSM agents and self-service portals.

modules.name: Problem Management
modules.description: Root-cause analysis, known-error tracking, and corrective action for recurring or major incidents.

modules.name: Service Request Fulfillment
modules.description: End-user-driven request workflow, including catalog authoring, request routing, approvals, and self-service portal.

modules.name: SLA and Chargeback Management
modules.description: SLA / OLA definitions, breach tracking, escalation rules, service-cost tracking, and chargeback to consuming business units.

domains.name: Workplace and Space Management
domains.description: Workplace booking, space optimisation, real-estate management, and visitor handling.

modules.name: Desk Reservation and Workplace Experience
modules.description: Hot-desking, hoteling, and desk-booking flows for hybrid-work programs. Masters `desk_bookings` and `workplace_experience_feedback`. The canonical entry-point module for return-to-office buyers.

modules.name: Location and Property Master
modules.description: Foundational location registry for an IWMS deployment. Canonically masters `locations` (sites, offices, branches, buildings, floors). Embedded-masters lease and property metadata where the IWMS holds the integrated record. Read by every other IWMS module as the spatial anchor.

modules.name: Meeting Room Reservation
modules.description: Conference room booking with calendar integration, room equipment (AV) tracking, no-show recapture, and check-in flows. Masters `room_reservations`. Often deployed alongside desk-reservation but has a distinct booking model (durations, attendees, recurring).

modules.name: Space Utilization and Occupancy Analytics
modules.description: Occupancy measurement, space-utilization reporting, stack diagrams, and scenario modelling for portfolio rationalization. Masters `space_utilization_reports`. Reads desk_bookings + room_reservations + sensor data; publishes utilization signals to REAL-EST and ESG.

modules.name: Workplace Service Requests
modules.description: Facility ticket flow: light-bulb out, temperature complaint, broken chair, spill cleanup. Masters `workplace_service_requests`. Distinct from ITSM (workplace-scoped, often dispatched to in-house facilities staff or building management) and from EAM/FSM (no equipment-asset orientation).

domains.name: Knowledge Graph Platform
domains.description: Enterprise platform for modeling, storing, querying, and reasoning over typed business objects via formal ontologies (OWL / RDF / SHACL) or labeled property graphs. Includes ontology authoring, inference, SPARQL/Cypher querying, virtual-graph federation, and operational-app affordances on top of the graph store. Buyer: enterprise architect / CDO / life-sciences R&D lead. Primary JTBD: build AI agents, operational apps, and complex analytical queries on top of a unified semantic model. Distinct from MDM (master-data orchestration without ontology engine), from DATA-AI-PLAT (broader scope including pipelines / lakehouse / ML), and from a plain graph database (KGP adds reasoning, ontology governance, and ontology-mapped operational-app affordances).

domains.name: Knowledge Management
domains.description: Authoring, governance, and retrieval of organisational knowledge for internal and external audiences.

domains.name: Kubernetes and Container Platforms
domains.description: Production Kubernetes distributions with day-2 operations, multi-cluster management, and integrated CI/CD.

domains.name: Low-Code Application Platform
domains.description: Visual and model-driven development of custom enterprise applications with managed runtime.

domains.name: Legal Practice Management
domains.description: Law-firm operational platform: matter management (case lifecycle, parties, key dates, deadlines), timekeeper time capture and billing (ABA UTBMS task codes), IOLTA trust-account ledgering, conflict-of-interest checking across matters and adversaries, client intake, engagement letters, and matter-scoped document management. Buyer is the law firm (solo to mid-size). Vendor unconstrained by formal certification, the attorney carries all regulatory weight under ABA Model Rules and state bar ethics.

modules.name: Court Calendaring and Docketing
modules.description: Court filing capture and submission, jurisdiction-specific deadline rule application, statute-of-limitations and key-date alerting, service-of-process tracking, and electronic court filing (ECF) integration.

modules.name: Client Intake and Conflict Clearance
modules.description: Intake forms, conflict-of-interest graph search across existing matters and adversaries, conflict-waiver and ethics-wall tracking, engagement-letter drafting from templates, retainer-payment collection, and intake-to-matter conversion.

modules.name: Matter Management
modules.description: Central matter lifecycle: intake-to-active-to-closed-to-archived, parties (clients, opposing, co-counsel, courts), key-date and statute-of-limitations alerting, matter status reporting, and matter-scoped document storage. The hub every other legal-practice module attaches to.

modules.name: Time Capture and Client Billing
modules.description: Timekeeper time-entry capture against ABA UTBMS task and expense codes, hourly versus flat-fee versus contingency versus alternative-fee-arrangement billing, prebill review and partner approval, e-bill submission in LEDES 1998B and 2000 formats, and AR aging on client invoices.

modules.name: IOLTA Trust Accounting
modules.description: Separated IOLTA trust-account ledger per client and matter, three-way reconciliation (bank versus ledger versus client-balance), state-specific retention rules, retainer-to-trust deposit and trust-to-operating transfer on earned fees, and state-bar reporting on exceptions.

domains.name: Library Management
domains.description: Software that manages physical and digital library collections, member services, circulation, acquisitions, cataloging, and discovery for public, academic, special, and school libraries.

modules.name: Acquisitions and Electronic Resources
modules.description: Ordering and receiving of physical materials, serials subscription management, and electronic resource license management.

modules.name: Cataloging and Metadata
modules.description: Authoring and maintenance of bibliographic, authority, and holdings records for all library materials regardless of medium. Owns the catalog substrate every other library module reads from.

modules.name: Circulation Operations
modules.description: Daily circulation activity: check-out, check-in, renewals, holds, fines, and circulation policy administration. The most patron-facing operations module.

modules.name: Discovery and OPAC
modules.description: Public-facing catalog search and browse interface, faceted search over bibliographic and holdings data, saved searches, and reading lists.

modules.name: Interlibrary Loan
modules.description: Borrowing and lending workflows between libraries, request routing, and resource sharing partner management.

modules.name: Patron Management
modules.description: Library member registration, profile maintenance, patron category administration, and card issuance.

domains.name: Learning Management
domains.description: Course authoring, content delivery, compliance training, certifications, and learning paths for employees and extended audiences.

modules.name: Compliance Training
modules.description: Mandatory regulatory training assignment, tracking, and certification: sexual harassment training (CA SB-1343), HIPAA, OSHA, anti-bribery, SOX, GDPR, AML. Masters `compliance_assignments` and `learner_certifications`. Realizes COMPLIANCE-TRAIN and CERT-MGMT. Distinct from general LMS course delivery: assignments are mandatory and time-bound, lifecycle includes `overdue`/`waived`/`expired` states with regulator-evidence retention, and ownership typically sits with GRC/Compliance, not L&D.

modules.name: Course Delivery
modules.description: The core LMS workflow: course authoring, content delivery, enrollment, completion tracking, and transcript posting. Masters `courses`, `course_enrollments`, `learning_records`. Realizes COURSE-AUTHOR and CONTENT-DELIVERY capabilities. The backbone module every LMS deployment installs first; the other LMS modules embedded_master courses to reference content.

modules.name: Skills and Learning Paths
modules.description: Skills-cloud surface of an LMS: employee skill profiles, competency tracking, and skills-driven learning-path recommendation. Masters `skill_profiles` and `learning_paths`. Realizes SKILLS-MGMT and LEARNING-PATH. Distinct from LMS-COURSE-DELIVERY because learning paths here are assigned to close skill gaps rather than sequenced as course curricula. Heavy contributors: TALENT-MGMT (talent reviews), ATS (internal mobility), SWP (workforce planning).

domains.name: Customer Loyalty Management
domains.description: Points/tiers, rewards programs, member portals, and partner ecosystems for B2C and B2B2C loyalty.

domains.name: Legal Service Delivery
domains.description: Intake, matter management, and operational workflows for the in-house legal function.

domains.name: Marketing Automation
domains.description: Email/SMS/cross-channel campaign orchestration, lead nurturing, and journey management. Includes B2B (account-based marketing) and B2C (consumer engagement) variants.

domains.name: Master Data Management
domains.description: Authoritative golden records for shared entities (customer, supplier, product, asset) including matching, survivorship, and stewardship.

domains.name: Metrics Layer / Headless BI
domains.description: Lightweight semantic tier defining metrics, dimensions, and entity relationships once, then exposing them via SQL/REST/GraphQL/MDX/JDBC to any downstream consumer: BI dashboards, AI agents, embedded analytics, custom apps. Distinct from BI (no UI / dashboard surface), from DI (no data movement), from DCG (no broader governance scope), and from DATA-AI-PLAT (a feature within those platforms, sold separately by pure-play vendors). Buyer: analytics / data-engineering leader.

domains.name: Manufacturing Connected Operations
domains.description: Shop-floor execution, work-instruction delivery, and operator-case management for manufacturers.

domains.name: MSP Professional Services Automation
domains.description: Ticketing, contracts, time tracking, billing, and resource scheduling built around the MSP business model: multi-tenant customer estates, recurring service contracts, block-hour and value-based pricing, and per-customer SLAs. Distinct from generic PSA (services-org variant for consultancies and agencies), different buyer, different competitive set.

modules.name: MSP Client Roster and Service Contracts
modules.description: MSP client roster and master service agreements with consumption tracking (block-hour decrement, value-tier pricing), renewal-window alerting, and contract-lifecycle gates for activation, suspension, and termination.

modules.name: Technician Dispatch and Scheduling
modules.description: Technician scheduling and dispatch board, skill-and-availability matching, on-site versus remote work routing, and load balancing across the technician roster.

modules.name: MSP Multi-Tenant Service Desk
modules.description: Multi-tenant service-ticket queue across customer estates, with per-customer SLA timers that pause and resume on hold, a customer portal for self-service ticket submission, and CSAT capture on resolution.

modules.name: MSP Time Tracking and Customer Billing
modules.description: Technician time-entry capture against tickets and contracts, time-approval routing, recurring billing per contract terms, and customer-side invoice issuance with AR-side aging from the MSP's perspective.

domains.name: No-Code Database
domains.description: Spreadsheet-database hybrids letting non-developers model structured data, link records across tables, build forms/views/dashboards, and automate without code. Bought self-serve by citizen developers in marketing, ops, HR, and PMO; expands to enterprise via Business/Enterprise tiers. Distinct from LCAP (no managed app runtime, no environments/version pipeline, no compiled UI), from BI (no warehouse, no analytical semantic layer), and from work management (the data model is the buy reason, not the project view).

domains.name: Network Performance Monitoring and Diagnostics
domains.description: Network observability, flow analysis, path tracing, and SaaS/internet performance monitoring across WAN, LAN, and cloud.

domains.name: Observability
domains.description: Unified metrics, logs, and traces for distributed systems, primarily targeting engineering teams.

domains.name: Order Management
domains.description: Order capture, orchestration, and fulfilment across channels and inventory pools.

domains.name: Employee Onboarding
domains.description: Orchestration of pre-boarding, Day-1, and 30/60/90 workflows for new hires, task lists, welcome experiences, documentation, e-signature, buddy assignments, and downstream provisioning coordination. Operates primarily as a downstream-trigger pattern: offer.accepted (ATS) and employee.created (HCM) drive journey instantiation, which then fans out events to ITSM (provisioning), IWMS (facilities), Payroll (activation), LMS (compliance training), HRSD (case escalation), and IGA (access provisioning), making it the textbook case for the cross_domain_handoffs analytic.

modules.name: Onboarding Document Intake
modules.description: Collection and validation of new-hire paperwork (I-9, tax, NDA, direct-deposit), with handoff to HCM/Payroll on completion.

modules.name: Onboarding Journey Management
modules.description: Plan templates, journey instances, stages, tasks, and cohorts. The core orchestration surface for new-hire onboarding programs.

modules.name: Onboarding Welcome Experience
modules.description: Pre-boarding communications, day-one welcome cadences, buddy pairing, and intro meeting coordination.

domains.name: Operational Resilience
domains.description: Mapping of important business services to people, processes, technology, and third parties, with tolerance setting.

domains.name: People Analytics
domains.description: HR metrics, dashboards, workforce composition analysis, attrition prediction, and DEI analytics on top of the HR data stack.

modules.name: DEI Analytics
modules.description: Representation analytics, pay-equity analysis, DEI dashboards, skill-gap signaling. No native master entities (computes over HCM, COMP-MGMT, and PA-WORKFORCE-METRICS data).

modules.name: Engagement Surveys
modules.description: Pulse and engagement-survey campaigns, driver-score computation, sentiment aggregation. Masters engagement_surveys; consumes survey_responses from EMP-EXP.

modules.name: Predictive Models
modules.description: Attrition-risk, flight-risk, succession-readiness, hire-success ML models. Masters attrition_forecasts and predictive_models; emits scored populations to TALENT-MGMT, HCM, and SWP.

modules.name: Workforce Metrics
modules.description: Headcount, attrition snapshots, span-of-control, workforce composition, and people-KPI scorecards. Masters people_kpis and workforce_segments. Entry-point module for People Analytics deployments.

domains.name: Payroll Management
domains.description: Gross-to-net calculation, statutory tax filing, garnishments, pay distribution, and multi-country payroll execution.

modules.name: Earnings, Deductions and Garnishments
modules.description: Catalog of earning codes, deduction codes, and court/agency garnishment orders that drive payroll calculation. Masters earning_codes, deduction_codes, garnishment_orders.

modules.name: Pay Slips and Self-Service Statements
modules.description: Generation, publishing, and employee self-service of pay slips, W2/1099/equivalent year-end statements. Masters pay_slips; reads from pay_runs.

modules.name: Payroll Run Execution
modules.description: Core pay-cycle execution: opens runs, ingests inputs (time, comp, benefits, expenses), performs gross-to-net calculation, books payroll journal entries, and triggers disbursement. Masters pay_runs and payroll_journal_entries.

modules.name: Statutory Tax Compliance
modules.description: Tax authority configuration, periodic statutory filings, year-end forms, and multi-jurisdiction tax rate management. Masters tax_authorities and tax_filings; consumes pay_runs for filing inputs.

domains.name: Product Information Management
domains.description: Centralized merchandising master for product attributes, descriptions, digital assets, taxonomies, translations, and channel-specific syndication. Distinct from PLM (engineering record), MDM (cross-entity golden records), and the commerce-platform-native catalog (runtime storefront view).

modules.name: Digital Assets for Products
modules.description: Image, video, 3D model, and document assets tied to products: ingestion, rights metadata, renditions, CDN distribution, and asset-to-product linking. Lives inside PIM in v1; can be split into its own DAM domain when warranted.

modules.name: Product Content and Attributes
modules.description: Core merchandising master: products, attributes, variants, categories, translations, supplier onboarding, compliance attributes, and the review-and-approve workflow before publish. The deploy entry-point for any PIM rollout.

modules.name: Channel Syndication
modules.description: Per-channel feed shaping and publish orchestration: marketplace seller feeds, search-engine merchant feeds, commerce-platform catalogs, retailer feeds, GDSN datapools. Validation against destination schemas; syndication-job state machine; rejected-listing remediation queue.

domains.name: Product Lifecycle Management
domains.description: Manages the digital thread of a physical product from concept through engineering release, manufacturing handoff, service, and end-of-life. Holds engineering BOMs, CAD data vault, part masters, engineering change orders, revisions, requirements traceability, configuration and variant rules, and the as-designed to as-built to as-maintained chain. Distinct from PROD-MGMT (software product management) and from MFG-OPS (shop-floor execution).

modules.name: CAD Data Vault
modules.description: Product Data Management module: vaulting, check-in / check-out, version control, and viewable generation for CAD files. Integrates with mechanical and electronic CAD authoring tools.

modules.name: Product Compliance
modules.description: Substance tracking and reporting for product regulations: RoHS, REACH, conflict minerals, PFAS. Captures supplier material declarations and generates compliance evidence per shipment / market.

modules.name: Engineering Core
modules.description: The foundational PLM module: part and item master, engineering BOM, revision control, and engineering change workflow. Every PLM rollout starts here.

modules.name: Manufacturing Process Management
modules.description: Bridges engineering release to the shop floor: mBOM authoring, manufacturing routing, work instructions, and configuration / variant resolution at order time.

modules.name: Requirements Management
modules.description: Requirements capture, decomposition, and traceability from customer / regulatory inputs through design, test, and validation. Used heavily in aerospace, automotive, and medical-device.

domains.name: Portfolio Monitoring for Private Capital
domains.description: KPI collection from portfolio companies, valuation tracking, performance metric computation (IRR/MOIC/TVPI/DPI), and LP-facing performance reporting for private capital funds.

modules.name: Fund Performance
modules.description: Time-weighted IRR/MOIC/TVPI/DPI computation across multi-vintage funds with PME benchmarking and J-curve normalization. Aggregate dashboards over portfolio holdings.

modules.name: LP Performance Reports
modules.description: Quarterly LP-facing performance reports with fund and individual position metrics. Distinct from FUND-ADMIN's PCAP statements (which are accounting-side capital-account ledgers); these are performance narratives.

modules.name: Portfolio Company Data
modules.description: KPI collection from portfolio companies via portal upload, email parsing, or accounting connectors. Also ESG and diversity metric collection for LP disclosure and SFDR compliance. Masters portfolio companies and the KPI / ESG record streams.

modules.name: Portfolio Valuations
modules.description: Fair-value marks per ASC 820 with methodology-tagged audit trail across reporting cycles. Distinct from CAP-TABLE's 409A valuations (which are issuer-side IRS valuations); these are LP-side fair-value marks.

domains.name: Privacy Management
domains.description: Data subject rights, consent, processing inventory, and privacy assessments.

domains.name: Partner Relationship Management
domains.description: Channel partner portals, deal registration, MDF management, partner training, and indirect-revenue analytics.

domains.name: Process Mining
domains.description: Event-log-driven discovery, conformance, and enhancement of business processes.

domains.name: Product Management
domains.description: Product discovery, customer-feedback aggregation, feature prioritization, roadmap planning, and release management for software products. Distinct from Strategic Portfolio Management (investment selection) and engineering issue tracking (delivery execution).

modules.name: Product Discovery and Prioritization
modules.description: Captures customer feedback and feature ideas, triages them, and prioritizes the backlog that feeds the roadmap. Covers feedback aggregation, opportunity and idea management, and feature prioritization (RICE, value-vs-effort, weighted scoring).

modules.name: Roadmap, Release, and Strategy
modules.description: Manages the product roadmap, plans and ships releases, runs beta programs, and tracks product metrics against strategy and OKRs. Bridges prioritized opportunities to delivered features and measured outcomes.

domains.name: Public-Sector Licensing and Permitting
domains.description: Application, review, issuance, inspection, and renewal of licenses and permits for governments.

domains.name: Professional Services Automation
domains.description: Resource planning and allocation, project accounting, project time and expense, project billing, and utilisation tracking for services organisations.

modules.name: Project Delivery
modules.description: Engagement scoping, project structure, tasks, milestones, deliverables, SLAs. The master of service_projects and project_tasks; consumes opportunities, contracts, and external work projects to ground the engagement.

modules.name: Project Financials
modules.description: Project budgets, billing-rate cards, invoice generation against milestones and time, revenue recognition tied to performance obligations. Masters project_billing_milestones; contributes to revenue_recognition_records ahead of the ERP GL post.

modules.name: Resource Management
modules.description: Resource pool, skills, demand-supply matching, project assignments and resource allocations, utilization forecasting. Masters project_assignments, resource_skill_inventories, project_resource_allocations.

modules.name: Time and Expense
modules.description: Timesheet capture, expense reporting, approval workflows for project work. Consumes time_entries and expense_reports owned by WFM and EXPENSE respectively; produces approved data for billing and cost posting.

domains.name: Real Estate Brokerage and Agent Operations
domains.description: Lead capture, transaction management (offer→close), MLS syndication, commission splits, tour scheduling, and broker CRM for residential and commercial brokerages. Buyer is the brokerage / individual agent (per-seat pricing) or the franchise (enterprise pricing). Distinct from generic CRM (deal model is the property transaction, not the sales opportunity; commissions are split across cooperating brokerages by MLS rules).

modules.name: Real Estate Agent Operations
modules.description: Agent-facing workflow from lead capture through closing. Lead nurture (writes back into CRM contributor flow), listing creation and MLS syndication, tour scheduling and execution, transaction management (offer to escrow to contingencies to close), and buyer/seller disclosure handling. The deployable unit for solo agents and small firms; agents in larger brokerages work here while broker oversight runs in BROKERAGE-OPS.

modules.name: Brokerage Oversight and Commission Management
modules.description: Broker-level oversight on top of agent operations. Multi-agent commission-split engine with franchise overrides and per-agent caps, broker compliance review of transactions and disclosures, trust-account / escrow oversight, broker-level MLS conformance review. Only deployed when the brokerage has grown past informal-broker-supervision scale (typically 10+ agents).

modules.name: Real Estate Agent (solo / small firm bundle)
modules.description: Solo-agent and small-firm persona bundle. Single-install deployable for individual real estate agents covering lead capture and contact relationships (CRM-LEAD-MGT, CRM-ACCT-MGT), listing creation and MLS syndication, tour scheduling, transaction execution, disclosure handling (RE-BROK-AGENT-OPS), and contract reference (CLM-REPOSITORY). One module to install; larger brokerages deploy the underlying full modules and skip the starter.

domains.name: Commercial Real Estate Operations
domains.description: CRE leasing pipeline (LOI→executed lease), CAM (common-area-maintenance) reconciliation, rent escalations, stacking plans, market-comp data, and tenant relations for commercial landlords and asset managers (office, retail, industrial, mixed-use). Buyer is the CRE owner/operator or third-party property manager. Distinct from RE-PROP-MGMT (residential lifecycle is operationally different, shorter leases, simpler accounting, no CAM).

domains.name: Real Estate Investment Management
domains.description: Fund accounting (carry, waterfall distributions), investor reporting (capital calls, statements), portfolio valuation (cap rates, DCF, NAV), acquisition underwriting, and NOI/IRR modeling for REITs, RE private-equity funds, and institutional asset managers. Buyer is the REIT or fund manager. Distinct from generic fund-accounting platforms by RE-specific asset modeling (cap rates, rent rolls, property-level cash flow) and partner accounting structures (LP/GP waterfalls).

domains.name: Residential Property Management
domains.description: Tenant screening, rent collection, maintenance-request portal, leasing pipeline, vacancy marketing, and per-property general ledger for residential landlords (single-family-rental managers, multifamily operators, condo/HOA management). Buyer is the property-management firm or self-managing landlord. Distinct from REAL-EST/IWMS (corporate-tenant view); this is the landlord-of-residential-units view.

domains.name: Real Estate and Workplace Management
domains.description: Umbrella for corporate real-estate, workplace optimization, facilities management, and building operations. Two market tiers: IWMS (enterprise) and CAFM (mid-market) — modeled as sub-domains via parent_domain_id. Covers properties, leases, spaces, occupancy, maintenance, capital projects, and utility consumption (Scope 1+2 emissions source for ESG).

domains.name: Remote Access and Support
domains.description: Attended (technician-initiated with end-user present) and unattended remote control of workstations and servers, with session recording, file transfer, privilege elevation, and NAT-traversal relay. Distinct from VDI (full virtual desktops), VPN (network-layer tunneling), and PAM (privileged-access management with credential vaulting).

domains.name: Retail Store Operations
domains.description: Task management, labour scheduling, and store-associate workflows for retail.

domains.name: Revenue Intelligence
domains.description: AI-driven analytics on sales activity, deal signals, and customer conversations to produce pipeline health, forecast accuracy, deal scoring, and rep coaching insights. Combines conversation intelligence (call/email recording and transcription) with auto-activity capture and revenue forecasting. Distinct from SALES-ENG (outbound cadence execution) and CRM (system-of-record for opportunities).

domains.name: Remote Monitoring and Management
domains.description: Agent-based monitoring, patching, scripting, and automation of distributed Windows/Mac/Linux endpoints and servers across multi-tenant customer estates. Sold to MSPs (per-endpoint pricing) and increasingly to internal IT departments (per-technician pricing). Distinct from ITOM (no full-stack APM/infrastructure observability), UEM (limited mobile/MDM coverage), and ITSM (no human-process workflow engine).

domains.name: Robotic Process Automation
domains.description: UI-level automation of repetitive, rule-based tasks across legacy and modern applications.

domains.name: Source-to-Pay
domains.description: End-to-end procurement workflows from sourcing through purchase order to payment.

domains.name: Sales Engagement
domains.description: Outbound sales cadences, multi-channel sequencing, dialler, conversation intelligence, and rep productivity. Sits alongside CRM rather than replacing it.

domains.name: Sales Performance Management
domains.description: Territory design, quota allocation, sales-capacity planning, incentive compensation management (ICM), commission calculation, plan modeling, payee statements, and SPIF programs for the sales organization. Code is SALES-PERF rather than the analyst-standard SPM because SPM is already used in this catalog for Strategic Portfolio Management (the IT/agile-delivery flavor). Distinct from COMP-MGMT (broad employee compensation: pay equity, bonus, equity admin) and from EPM (finance-led planning).

domains.name: Software Asset Management
domains.description: Discovery, normalisation, entitlement reconciliation, and audit defence for software licences.

domains.name: Security Operations
domains.description: Detection, response, and remediation of security incidents, vulnerabilities, and threats.

domains.name: Strategy Execution Management
domains.description: Strategy-office software for defining strategic objectives, cascading them through the org, tracking strategic initiatives, running operating cadences (QBRs/MBRs), and maintaining traceability from execution back to strategy. Bought by Chief of Staff / Chief Strategy Officer / Strategy Office, distinct from HR-led Talent Management (performance reviews + goals bundled with HCM), from PMO-led Strategic Portfolio Management (IT/agile portfolio delivery), and from finance-led Enterprise Performance Management (budgeting).

modules.name: Execution Tracking
modules.description: Strategic initiative registers, progress capture, traceability between execution work and strategic intent, and investment-alignment views.

modules.name: Operating Rhythm
modules.description: Operating-cadence orchestration (QBR / MBR / weekly business reviews), agenda templates, structured commentary capture, action-item and decision tracking.

modules.name: Strategy Definition
modules.description: Authoring of strategic objectives, strategy maps, and the cascading hierarchy from corporate strategy down to team-level OKRs.

domains.name: Skills Management
domains.description: Substrate layer for the worker-skill graph: ingests skill signals from learning, performance, and recruiting; runs inference and gap analysis against curated taxonomies and competency models; serves a canonical skill profile per worker to downstream talent, planning, and intelligence consumers.
