modules.name: Worker Skill Profiles and Assessments
modules.description: Maintains per-worker skill profiles, runs the inference pipeline, captures assessments and validations, and produces gap and inventory analytics.

modules.name: Skill Taxonomy and Competency Models
modules.description: Authors the skill ontology, the role-to-skill competency bundles, and the source-of-truth references that other modules consume.

domains.name: Social Media Management
domains.description: Cross-channel social media publishing, scheduling, engagement (unified inbox), listening, analytics, and influencer/advocacy programs. Distinct from MA (lifecycle marketing automation), CDP (customer data unification), and SALES-ENG (1:1 outbound sales engagement).

domains.name: SaaS Management Platforms
domains.description: Discovery, optimisation, lifecycle management, and renewal management for the company's SaaS portfolio. Distinct from classic SAM which focuses on installed software licences.

modules.name: SMP Discovery and Optimization
modules.description: Discovery of sanctioned and shadow SaaS, usage analytics, license rightsizing, and provisioning lifecycle automation. The operational substrate of an SMP deployment.

modules.name: SMP Renewal and Vendor Management
modules.description: Renewal pipeline tracking, vendor relationship management, and contract coordination with procurement and legal. The commercial layer on top of the discovery and optimization substrate.

domains.name: Security Orchestration, Automation and Response
domains.description: Playbook-driven automation of security operations workflows across alerts and case management.

domains.name: Corporate Spend Management
domains.description: Unified employee-initiated spend across corporate cards, expense reimbursement, vendor bill pay, and pre-spend approval, anchored on real-time card authorization and policy enforcement at the network layer. Distinct from EXPENSE (post-hoc reimbursement / reconciliation of bank-issued cards), S2P (sourcing-led enterprise procurement with three-way-match), and AP-AUTO (invoice OCR + match + payment).

domains.name: Strategic Portfolio Management
domains.description: Top-down planning of investments, demand, resources, and agile delivery across portfolios.

domains.name: Subscription and Revenue Lifecycle Management
domains.description: Recurring billing, subscription lifecycle (upgrades, downgrades, suspensions), revenue recognition, and dunning for subscription and usage-based business models.

modules.name: HVAC Service Management (small-org starter)
modules.description: Small-shop persona bundle for HVAC service businesses (5-30 technicians). Single-install deployable covering customer accounts and contacts (CRM), service dispatch and field execution (FSM-DISPATCH-OPS), installed equipment register and PM cadence (FSM-INSTALLED-BASE), recurring maintenance agreements (FSM-SERVICE-CONTRACTS), quote-to-invoice (CPQ + SUB-MGMT), and parts inventory (HAM). Larger organisations deploy the full modules and skip the starter.

modules.name: Recurring Billing and Dunning
modules.description: Invoice generation, payment processing, tax calculation, and dunning workflows for failed-payment recovery. Sits downstream of the subscription lifecycle.

modules.name: Subscription Lifecycle and Usage
modules.description: Subscription record lifecycle (active, paused, cancelled, churned), usage metering and rating, revenue recognition under ASC 606 / IFRS 15, entitlement management, and SaaS churn analytics. The recurring-revenue substrate.

domains.name: Supplier Lifecycle Management
domains.description: Supplier onboarding, master data, performance, and relationship management.

domains.name: Strategic Workforce Planning
domains.description: Multi-year strategic capacity planning: target headcount, skills, location, and cost over a 1-5 year horizon, with what-if scenarios (growth, reduction, restructure, geography shift, skill pivot, cost optimization) and labor-market overlay. Distinct from WFM (operational scheduling, time and attendance) and EPM (financial planning) although it produces a cost projection that EPM consumes. Operates upstream of ATS: SWP authorises hiring (headcount.approved triggers requisition creation), making SWP and ATS co-masters of job_requisitions, the canonical multi-master Signal-1 example.

modules.name: Cost Projections
modules.description: Workforce cost projections, cost-of-plan, and the EPM handoff. Masters workforce_cost_projections (the boundary-object SWP authors and EPM consumes into the consolidated budget). Contributor on financial_plans for the labor-cost slice. Consumes salary_bands, merit_cycles, cost_centers, employees, and hcm_positions to size the loaded-comp envelope.

modules.name: Demand Forecast
modules.description: Headcount and position demand modeling. Masters headcount_plans, position_demand_forecasts, and skills_gap_analyses. Publishes the canonical hiring-demand signal that ATS, HCM, and LMS subscribe to. Contributor on the requisition slice of job_requisitions (the authorization-to-hire layer that ATS then executes).

modules.name: Scenario Modeling
modules.description: What-if workforce scenarios, org-design alternatives, labor-market intelligence, and M&A or transformation models. Masters workforce_plans, workforce_scenarios, org_designs, and labor_market_benchmarks. Adopted scenarios cascade into HCM org changes, EPM financial-plan assumptions, and downstream cost projections.

modules.name: Supply Planning
modules.description: Talent supply, internal-mobility forecasts, retirement curves, and gap reconciliation. Consumes employees, hcm_positions, org_units, job_profiles, attrition_forecasts, and skills_gap_analyses to build the supply side of the workforce plan. No unique master entities today; supply artifacts are derived from consumed sources and feed back into demand-vs-supply reconciliation inside the planning cycle.

domains.name: Talent Management
domains.description: Performance reviews, goals and OKRs, continuous feedback, succession planning, and career development. Often bundled into HCM suites but a strong standalone point-solution market exists.

modules.name: Continuous Feedback and Recognition
modules.description: Continuous feedback, 1-on-1 notes, peer recognition / praise / kudos, and 360-degree feedback collection. The lightweight always-on companion to the annual review cycle. Often the first module customers adopt before a full review cycle.

modules.name: Performance and Goal Management
modules.description: Annual / semi-annual / continuous performance review cycles plus goal-setting (OKRs, KPIs) and rating calibration. The foundation module of any talent-management deployment; review cycles produce the calibrated rating that COMP-MGMT consumes for merit cycles and that downstream succession planning relies on.

modules.name: Succession and Career Planning
modules.description: Succession planning, 9-box talent grid, and employee career aspirations / development planning. Layered on top of Performance Management because it consumes calibrated ratings and goal-completion outcomes. Used standalone in large enterprises with formal succession programs.

domains.name: Telecommunications OSS/BSS
domains.description: Operations and business support systems for service providers: catalog, order, inventory, billing.

domains.name: Vehicle Telematics
domains.description: GPS tracking, ELD (electronic logging device) compliance, driver-behavior monitoring (harsh braking, speeding, cornering), video telematics (dashcam), and engine/vehicle sensor data ingestion. The connectivity-and-data layer for fleet operations; foundation for utilization analytics, maintenance triggers, and incident-claims evidence. Distinct from FLEET-MGMT (the workflow layer above) and from generic IoT platforms (this is vehicle-specific hardware-plus-software).

domains.name: Test Management
domains.description: Authoring, execution, and tracking of manual and automated software tests.

domains.name: Threat Intelligence
domains.description: Collection, curation, and operationalisation of indicators and adversary intelligence.

domains.name: Talent Intelligence
domains.description: AI matching and recommendation layer that runs over skill profiles, roles, and opportunities to drive internal mobility, succession readiness, and skills-based hiring decisions. Owns the talent marketplace, the recommendation surfaces, and the audit trail of model inferences; defers person and skill mastership to HCM and SKILLS-MGMT.

modules.name: Workforce Skills Insights
modules.description: Org-level skills heatmaps, gap and supply analytics for HR leaders.

modules.name: Talent Marketplace
modules.description: Operates the internal opportunity marketplace: posting, matching, and applying across gigs, projects, full-time internal moves, and mentorships.

modules.name: Mobility, Succession and Fit
modules.description: AI recommendations, fit scores, succession ranking, career-path projection, and skills-based hiring assist.

domains.name: Third-Party Risk Management
domains.description: Onboarding, due diligence, and ongoing risk monitoring of vendors and third parties.

domains.name: Unified Endpoint Management
domains.description: Modern device management across Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, and ChromeOS, including MDM, configuration, app distribution, and zero-touch provisioning.

domains.name: Utilities Operations
domains.description: Meter-to-cash, outage, work, and asset workflows for electric, gas, and water utilities.

domains.name: Veterinary Practice Management
domains.description: Veterinary-clinic operational platform: animal-patient records, pet-owner relationship management, appointment scheduling (species/exam-type fit), examination notes, vaccination and reminder protocols, drug inventory and controlled-substance ledger, lab-results integration, in-clinic POS, and boarding/grooming scheduling. Buyer is the veterinary clinic or hospital, solo to multi-site. No HIPAA exposure (animals aren't covered entities); DEA Controlled Substances Act applies to e-prescribing modules.

domains.name: Visitor Management
domains.description: Pre-registration, check-in, badging, and tracking of physical-site visitors.

domains.name: Vendor Management System
domains.description: Contingent-workforce sourcing, staffing-supplier management, rate cards, contingent timesheets, and worker-classification compliance. Used by procurement and HR jointly.

domains.name: Value Stream Delivery Platform
domains.description: End-to-end DevOps platforms covering source control, CI/CD, build, test, release, and value-stream analytics.

domains.name: Vulnerability Management
domains.description: Identification, prioritisation, and remediation of security vulnerabilities across endpoints, cloud, and applications.

domains.name: Web Content Operations & Governance
domains.description: Editorial workflow, content audit and inventory, accessibility scanning, content quality / brand-voice enforcement, technical SEO governance, broken-link monitoring, and content-lifecycle planning for external / customer-facing web properties. The people-and-process layer over DXP and HCMS. Buyer is Marketing or the Web team. Distinct from INTRANET (internal audience), DXP (the delivery platform), and DAM (the asset store).

domains.name: Workforce Management
domains.description: Time and attendance tracking, shift scheduling, absence and leave, labour forecasting, and labour-law compliance for hourly and shift-based workforces.

domains.name: Work Management
domains.description: Task, project, and workflow management for cross-functional work: assignments with owners and due dates, dependencies, statuses, dashboards, automations, and goal/OKR tracking. Bought by PMOs, BizOps, marketing, and any team running structured work that isn't engineering-specific. Distinct from SPM (top-down portfolio investment decision, CIO/EPMO buyer), from PSA (services billing/utilization for revenue-generating projects), and from dedicated software-engineering sprint tooling (developer-specific dev workflow).

modules.name: Team-Execution Goals and OKRs
modules.description: Team-execution OKR tracking surface: objectives with key results that link to work items for automatic progress rollup, weekly check-in cadences, scoring, and closure. Deploys alongside the task-execution module for full integration, or standalone with a thin embedded work-item shell for KR linking.

modules.name: Task and Project Execution
modules.description: Cross-functional task and project execution surface: work items with owners, due dates, dependencies, statuses, and assignments; project containers with timelines, dashboards, and board views; user-authored automation rules that fire on item state changes. The deployable substrate every team-based work-management product orbits.

domains.name: Workstream Collaboration
domains.description: Persistent channel-based messaging, threaded conversations, voice/video huddles, and external collaboration. Distinct from KMS (knowledge artifacts).

modules.name: Channels and Conversations
modules.description: The core conversational surface of a workstream collaboration platform: persistent channels, messages, threads, channel membership, file attachments, and presence. Hosts the search/retention and chat-to-work-escalation features that live at the channel/message layer.

modules.name: External Collaboration
modules.description: External guest invitations, scoped access into channels for partners and vendors, and access-revocation workflow on guest offboarding.

modules.name: Huddles and Voice Drop-Ins
modules.description: Voice and video huddles anchored to channels or DMs; ad-hoc and scheduled forms.
